triageFlags.title: Credit Score Below Minimum Threshold
triageFlags.description: The applicant's credit score is below the minimum threshold for approval.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application unless exceptional circumstances are documented.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, a credit score below 550 results in automatic decline unless exceptional circumstances are documented. The applicant's score of 549 does not meet the minimum requirement.

triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Ratio Exceeds Maximum for Credit Tier
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio exceeds the maximum allowable for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider revising the loan structure to comply with the LTV policy.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum LTV of 95% for applicants in the 'Sub-Prime' credit tier. The current LTV of 91.11% exceeds this limit, indicating a policy violation.

content.reasoning: The applicant's credit score of 549 places them in the 'Decline' tier, which typically results in automatic rejection unless exceptional circumstances are documented. Additionally, the Loan-to-Value ratio of 91.11% exceeds the maximum allowable 95% for their credit tier, further justifying the recommendation to decline.
